samples.xml_description:
<module id="signal_generator">
    <description>Triangle Wave signal generator.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <output id="wave" width_description="[4:0]">Output waveform signal.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <param id="state">State register for controlling the waveform generation.</param>
        <param id="wave">Waveform register representing the generated signal.</param>
    </param_description>
    <implementation>
        The module generates a triangle waveform by incrementing and decrementing a 5-bit signal named "wave."
        It cycles through the waveform by determining the current state, either incrementing or decrementing,
        and continuously produces the waveform without additional control signals.
        The state and waveform registers are updated in the always block triggered on the positive edge of the clock
        and the negative edge of the reset signal. The state register controls the waveform generation, while the
        waveform register represents the generated signal. On reset, the state is set to 0, and the waveform is
        initialized to 0. The state transitions based on the current values of the waveform, incrementing or
        decrementing the waveform to generate the triangle waveform.
    </implementation>
</module>
